summary: The slash command gates on `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml`, delegates assessment to a fresh `zoto-spec-judge` subagent using `zoto-judge-spec`, supports repository-wide and spec-targeted modes with dated Markdown reports, and after spec runs may offer spec-only remediation.

cases.scenario: Abort when Spec System config is absent
cases.expected_output: The invocation stops before any spawned judge runs, emitting only the mandated spec-system initialisation error and leaving no new assessment markdown behind.
assertions: When `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml` is absent at the repository root, the command does not spawn `zoto-spec-judge` and does not synthesise a replacement config file.
The surfaced text is exactly: Spec System is not initialised. Run `/z-spec-init` first to create `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml`.
After `/z-spec-judge`, the manifest records the `z-spec-judge` attempt and shows no completed hand-off to `zoto-spec-judge` because execution halted during precondition checking.
Judge assessments save under filenames matching `assessment-repo-<YYYYMMDD>.md` or `assessment-<spec-slug>-<YYYYMMDD>.md`.
Assessments score each spec against the documented rubric headings: `Completeness`, `Feasibility`, `Structure`, `Specificity`, `Risk Awareness`, and `Convention Compliance`.
The verdict line emits one of `Approve`, `Conditional`, or `Reject`.

cases.scenario: Repo-wide assessment writes rubric-backed report under specsDir
cases.expected_output: A fresh repository assessment report appears under the configured specs directory using the assessment-repo date-stamped filename pattern and summarises health across the six mandated review axes.
fixture_justifications: Creates `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml` at the workspace root so the precondition passes and Mode 1 can run.
assertions: After `/z-spec-judge` with no arguments, a new `zoto-spec-judge` subagent starts in isolation and receives empty `$ARGUMENTS` for the full-repo pass.
The subagent executes through the `zoto-judge-spec` skill while walking agents, skills, commands, hooks, automated tests, scripts, and configuration layout.
A markdown file is written to `{specsDir}/assessment-repo-[yyyymmdd].md` using the repo’s configured `{specsDir}` segment.
The written analysis visibly covers completeness, feasibility, structure, specificity, risk awareness, and convention compliance before stating any readiness opinion.
After `/z-spec-judge`, the manifest MUST record at least two targets tying the palette command to the repository assessment outcome.
Judge assessments save under filenames matching `assessment-repo-<YYYYMMDD>.md` or `assessment-<spec-slug>-<YYYYMMDD>.md`.
Assessments score each spec against the documented rubric headings: `Completeness`, `Feasibility`, `Structure`, `Specificity`, `Risk Awareness`, and `Convention Compliance`.
The verdict line emits one of `Approve`, `Conditional`, or `Reject`.

cases.scenario: Directory argument auto-finds index and audits spec depth
cases.expected_output: The spec assessment finishes, askQuestion prompts whether to apply structured fixes, accepted changes touch solely spec-side documents, and the assessment file records which remediations landed.

# Circuit breaker HTTP API

## Objectives
Deliver a resilient edge proxy that sheds load when upstream latency spikes.

## Deliverables
- OpenAPI description for the control plane
- Contract tests for timeout and half-open behaviour

## Dependency graph
- subtask-01-circuit-breaker-api-scaffold-20260403.md has no upstream dependencies

## Subtasks
- [subtask-01-circuit-breaker-api-scaffold-20260403.md](subtask-01-circuit-breaker-api-scaffold-20260403.md)

---
title: Scaffold service skeleton
---

# Scaffold service skeleton

## Objective
Establish the module layout and configuration loading path.

## Deliverables
- Runnable entrypoint
- Configuration schema

## Agent assignment
Platform engineer agent

fixture_justifications: Initialises Spec System so the command may proceed past the config gate.
Provides a spec index file matching `spec-*.md` plus objectives so directory mode can locate the tree automatically.
Provides a linked subtask so subtask-quality and dependency checks have concrete material beyond the index page alone.
assertions: For directory-scoped spec assessments the judge surfaces actionable findings and issues `askQuestion` before altering any spec index, subtask file, or dependency graph artifact.
When the operator accepts, modifications stay limited to spec markdown and dependency metadata—runtime service code and libraries remain untouched.
The assessment markdown is revised to spell out which automated fixes executed so reviewers see a complete audit trail.
After `/z-spec-judge`, the manifest MUST capture the `askQuestion` prompt prior to logging any write operations against spec paths.
Judge assessments save under filenames matching `assessment-repo-<YYYYMMDD>.md` or `assessment-<spec-slug>-<YYYYMMDD>.md`.
Assessments score each spec against the documented rubric headings: `Completeness`, `Feasibility`, `Structure`, `Specificity`, `Risk Awareness`, and `Convention Compliance`.
The verdict line emits one of `Approve`, `Conditional`, or `Reject`.
follow_ups: Proceed with the remediation: tighten the listed subtasks, repair the dependency edges, and refresh the assessment appendix to list the edits you applied.

cases.scenario: Index file argument targets one spec tree
cases.expected_output: Assessment is anchored on the supplied index file, keeps generated notes within the same spec directory hierarchy, and confines automatic edits later to spec artefacts only.

# Scaffold service skeleton

## Objective
Establish the module layout and configuration loading path.

## Deliverables
- Runnable entrypoint
- Configuration schema

## Agent assignment
Platform engineer agent

fixture_justifications: Supplies the required Spec System config file so execution is not blocked at the gate.
Gives a concrete `spec-*.md` path matching the file-argument mode in the usage section.
Includes a dependent subtask file so the judge can verify linked content when given an explicit index path.
assertions: After `/z-spec-judge specs/20260403-feature-name/spec-feature-name-20260403.md`, judgement work uses that explicit index path when resolving the spec package.
Report output remains co-located with the spec directory housing the named index instead of drifting into unrelated product modules.
Passive review inspects implementation areas only for verification and does not rewrite application source files before any optional fix offer.
After `/z-spec-judge`, the manifest MUST echo the precise file argument so traceability matches the operator-supplied path.
Judge assessments save under filenames matching `assessment-repo-<YYYYMMDD>.md` or `assessment-<spec-slug>-<YYYYMMDD>.md`.
Assessments score each spec against the documented rubric headings: `Completeness`, `Feasibility`, `Structure`, `Specificity`, `Risk Awareness`, and `Convention Compliance`.
The verdict line emits one of `Approve`, `Conditional`, or `Reject`.

cases.scenario: Operator accepts spec-only remediation after assessment
follow_ups: Yes—apply the recommended edits only inside this spec folder’s Markdown and refresh the assessment note afterward.
cases.expected_output: Following approval, edits remain confined to spec artefacts in the assessed directory and the assessment report records which corrections were applied, without touching paths outside that spec scope.

# Scaffold service skeleton

## Objective
Establish the module layout and configuration loading path.

## Deliverables
- Runnable entrypoint
- Configuration schema

## Agent assignment
Platform engineer agent

fixture_justifications: Ensures the command is allowed to run by providing the Spec System configuration file.
Seeds the spec index that will receive judge recommendations so acceptance has defined targets.
Seeds a subtask document so remediation can clarify deliverables without inventing new paths.
assertions: After the follow-up approving work, the recorded file-write operations do not touch paths outside `specs/20260403-circuit-breaker-api/`.
After remediation completes, the assessment Markdown in that directory states which spec edits were applied, satisfying the documented post-fix logging expectation.
